title: A geometric Fano--Procrustes framework for purification-based distances and quantum channels analysis
abstract: In this work we reformulate the Uhlmann purification-overlap optimization and develop a purification-based geometric framework for the analysis of mixed qubit states and qubit channels. Using the Fano representation of two-qubit pure states, a purification is described in terms of the Bloch vector of the system, the ancilla Bloch vector, and a real correlation matrix. For a fixed one-qubit mixed state, the freedom in the choice of purification can be parametrized by proper rotations acting on the ancillary degrees of freedom. As a result, the optimization over purifications entering the definition of the metric \(D_N\) introduced in Ref.~\cite{Lamberti2009} is reduced to an orthogonal Procrustes problem on the Lie group \(SO(3)\). This reduction yields not only the maximal purification overlap, but also the optimal rotation relating the purification frames. From this rotation we define a purification misalignment angle \(Θ\), which provides geometric information not contained in scalar fidelity-based distinguishability measures.   The formalism is applied to representative qubit channels, including depolarizing, bit-flip, phase-flip, amplitude-damping channels, and an imperfect quantum NOT gate. For symmetry-adapted evolutions preserving the Bloch-vector direction, the optimal rotation is trivial and \(Θ=0\), whereas noncollinear channel actions generate a nonzero misalignment. The pair \((D_N,Θ)\) therefore separates the magnitude of the maximal purification overlap from the geometric reorientation of the optimal purification frames. Since the optimal Procrustes rotation can be lifted to a local unitary acting on the ancilla, the construction also provides an operational interpretation of the optimal purification in terms of an ancilla-side transformation.
summary: This paper introduces a new geometric framework to analyze quantum state distinguishability by looking beyond scalar fidelity. By reducing purification optimization to a Procrustes problem, it identifies a misalignment angle that captures the geometric reorientation of quantum channels. This allows for a more detailed distinction between purely dissipative and anisotropic noise processes.
main_problem: The paper addresses the limitation of existing fidelity-based distance measures that provide only a scalar value for distinguishability, discarding the underlying geometric structure of optimal purifications.
main_result: The authors develop a Fano-Procrustes framework that reduces purification-overlap optimization to an orthogonal Procrustes problem on SO(3), introducing a new geometric misalignment angle (Theta) that complements scalar metrics.
method: The work uses the Fano representation of two-qubit pure states and applies Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) to solve the orthogonal Procrustes problem on the Lie group SO(3).
model_or_system: The framework is applied to mixed qubit states and various quantum channels, including depolarizing, bit-flip, phase-flip, and amplitude-damping channels, as well as an imperfect quantum NOT gate.
key_observables: Maximal purification overlap (g*), entropic metric (DN), purification misalignment angle (Theta), and the optimal Procrustes rotation (S*).
important_parameters: Channel error probabilities (p), damping probability (gamma), and Bloch vector length (r).
main_assumptions: The current framework is specifically developed and restricted to the qubit (two-level) case.
figures_summary: Figures show the behavior of the DN metric and the misalignment angle Theta as functions of error probabilities and input state orientations for various noise channels.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of purification geometry, develops the Fano-Procrustes mathematical framework, applies it to specific qubit channels to demonstrate the separation of radial and anisotropic effects, and concludes with discussions on the operational interpretation and limitations.
why_interesting: It provides a new geometric diagnostic (Theta) for characterizing the anisotropic and non-radial components of quantum noise, which is highly relevant for characterizing open quantum systems and control errors.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. The metric DN(ρ, ΦBF p (ρ)) as a function of the bit- flip probability p, for fixed Bloch angles and different values of the Bloch-vector length.
FIG. 3. The metric DN(ρ, ΦBF p (ρ)) as a function of p, for a fixed Bloch-vector length and different orientations of the input state.

FIG. 4. Purification misalignment angle Θ(ρ, ΦBF p (ρ)) as a function of p, for the same configurations as in Fig. 3. The orientation dependence reflects the anisotropic nature of the bit-flip channel.

FIG. 2. Purification misalignment angle Θ(ρ, ΦBF p (ρ)) as a function of the bit-flip probability p, for the same configu- rations as in Fig. 1. The angle captures the geometric re- alignment of the optimal purification frames induced by the anisotropic channel action.
FIG. 5. The metric DN(ρ, ΦPF p (ρ)) as a function of the phase- flip probability p, for fixed Bloch angles and different values of the Bloch-vector length.
